2604.12743.title: Can AI Tools Transform Low-Demand Math Tasks? An Evaluation of Task Modification Capabilities
2604.12743.paper_summary: While recent research has explored AI tools' ability to classify the quality of mathematical tasks (arXiv:2603.03512), little is known about their capacity to increase the quality of existing tasks. This study investigated whether AI tools could successfully upgrade low-cognitive-demand mathematics tasks. Eleven tools were tested, including six broadly available, general-purpose AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT and Claude) and five tools specialized for mathematics teachers (e.g., Khanmigo, coteach.ai). Using the Task Analysis Guide framework (Stein & Smith, 1998), we prompted AI tools to modify two different types of low-demand mathematical tasks. The prompting strategy aimed to represent likely approaches taken by knowledgeable teachers, rather than extensive optimization to find a more effective prompt (i.e., an optimistic typical outcome). On average, AI tools were only moderately successful: tasks were accurately upgraded only 64% of the time, with different AI tool performance ranging from quite weak (33%) to broadly successful (88%). Specialized tools were only moderately more successful than general-purpose tools. Failure modes included both "undershooting" (maintaining low cognitive demand) and "overshooting" (elevating tasks to an overly ambitious target category that likely would be rejected by teachers). Interestingly, there was a small negative correlation (r = -.35) between whether a given AI tool was able to correctly classify the cognitive demand of tasks and whether the AI was able to upgrade tasks, showing that the ability to modify tasks (i.e., a generative task) represents a distinct capability from the ability to classify them (i.e., judgement using a rubric). These findings have important implications for understanding AI's potential role in curriculum adaptation and highlight the need for specialized approaches to support teachers in modifying instructional materials.
2604.12743.paper_summary_zh: 最近的研究探討了人工智慧工具在分類數學任務質量方面的能力（arXiv:2603.03512），但對於它們提高現有任務質量的能力知之甚少。這項研究調查了人工智慧工具是否能成功升級低認知需求的數學任務。測試了十一種工具，包括六種廣泛可用的通用人工智慧工具（例如，ChatGPT 和 Claude）以及五種專為數學教師設計的工具（例如，Khanmigo、coteach.ai）。使用任務分析指南框架（Stein & Smith, 1998），我們提示人工智慧工具修改兩種不同類型的低需求數學任務。提示策略旨在代表知識淵博的教師可能採取的方式，而不是進行廣泛的優化以尋找更有效的提示（即，樂觀的典型結果）。平均而言，人工智慧工具的成功率僅為中等：任務準確升級的次數僅為64%，不同人工智慧工具的表現範圍從相當薄弱（33%）到廣泛成功（88%）。專業工具的成功率僅比通用工具稍高。失敗模式包括“低估”（保持低認知需求）和“高估”（將任務提升到過於雄心勃勃的目標類別，這可能會被教師拒絕）。有趣的是，給定的人工智慧工具能否正確分類任務的認知需求與人工智慧是否能升級任務之間存在小的負相關（r = -.35），顯示修改任務的能力（即，生成任務）與分類任務的能力（即，使用評分標準進行判斷）是不同的能力。這些發現對於理解人工智慧在課程適應中的潛在角色具有重要意義，並強調了支持教師修改教學材料的專業方法的必要性。
2604.12743.author: Danielle S. Fox et.al.
2604.12743.authors: Danielle S. Fox, Brenda L. Robles, Elizabeth DiPietro Brovey, Christian D. Schunn
